questions.text: Why does the Surah open with an oath by the nightcomer star?
questions.answer: To draw attention and symbolize watchfulness and the piercing clarity of truth.

questions.text: True/False: Surah At-Tariq teaches that humans are unobserved and unaccountable.
questions.answer: False. It teaches every soul has a guardian and deeds are not ignored.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach by asking humans to look at their origin?
questions.answer: Humility and certainty: the One who created from a humble origin can resurrect.

questions.text: How does Surah At-Tariq connect resurrection to accountability of the heart?
questions.answer: It says secrets will be exposed, showing judgment is based on inner reality and intention.

questions.text: What is one practical way to prepare for the exposure of secrets?
questions.answer: Purify intentions, avoid hidden sins, and make sincere repentance regularly.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that a person will have helpers that can cancel their sins on the Day of Judgment.
questions.answer: False. It says a person will have no power or helper in that moment.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about the sky that ‘returns’ and earth that ‘splits’?
questions.answer: They are signs of Allah’s repeated provision and power, supporting faith and reflection.

questions.text: How does calling the Quran a ‘decisive word’ change how we treat it?
questions.answer: We must take it seriously as guidance and judgment, not as entertainment or opinion.

questions.text: What is one modern example of treating the Quran as ‘amusement’?
questions.answer: Consuming it only for aesthetics while refusing to follow its commands or reform behavior.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that opponents of truth may plot, but Allah’s plan prevails.
questions.answer: True.

questions.text: How does Surah At-Tariq comfort the Prophet and believers facing opposition?
questions.answer: It reminds that enemies plot, but Allah also plans, and Allah’s plan is stronger.

questions.text: What does the command to ‘give them respite’ teach about da'wah strategy?
questions.answer: Remain patient and trust Allah’s timing; guidance and judgment are not rushed by emotion.

questions.text: How can a believer apply patience when facing mockery or pressure?
questions.answer: Stay calm, keep obedience, avoid reactive sin, and continue with wisdom and good character.

questions.text: How does the Surah strengthen belief in Allah’s power over life and death?
questions.answer: It shows Allah creates, provides, observes, and can resurrect with ease.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that human dignity comes from self-made achievement, not from being created by Allah.
questions.answer: False. It points to creation as a reason for humility and dependence.

questions.text: What is one lesson about intention (niyyah) from the Surah’s focus on secrets?
questions.answer: Intentions matter greatly; Allah judges inner motives alongside outward deeds.

questions.text: How can someone strengthen sincerity based on this Surah?
questions.answer: Increase private worship, avoid showing off, and regularly ask Allah to purify the heart.

questions.text: What does the Surah teach about human overconfidence in power or allies?
questions.answer: On the Day of exposure, no worldly power or helper can save a person.

questions.text: How does Surah At-Tariq teach the believer to value truth over popularity?
questions.answer: It calls the Quran decisive and shows plots against truth fail before Allah.

questions.text: True/False: The Surah teaches that Allah’s watchfulness should lead to despair.
questions.answer: False. It should lead to accountability, repentance, and hope in Allah’s guidance.

questions.text: What does 'At-Tariq' mean?
questions.answer: The Nightcomer or the Bright Star that appears at night.

questions.text: What is the 'Piercing Star' (An-Najm al-Thaqib)?
questions.answer: It is the interpretation of At-Tariq; a star so bright it seems to pierce through the darkness.

questions.text: Is there any soul without a guardian/protector over it?
questions.answer: No, every soul has over it a protector/guardian by Allah’s command.

questions.text: What does Allah ask man to observe in this Surah?
questions.answer: To observe from what he was created.

questions.text: What liquid was man created from?
questions.answer: From a 'gushing fluid' (Ma'in dafiq).

questions.text: From where does this fluid emerge according to this Surah?
questions.answer: From between the backbone and the ribs.

questions.text: Is Allah able to return the human to life after death?
questions.answer: Yes, Allah is able to return him to life.

questions.text: What will happen to 'secrets' on the Day of Judgment?
questions.answer: The secrets will be 'put to trial' or exposed (Tubla al-Sara'ir).

questions.text: Will man have any power or helper on that Day?
questions.answer: No, he will have neither power nor helper.

questions.text: Allah swears by the 'sky which returns'. What does 'returning' refer to?
questions.answer: It refers to the rain that returns repeatedly, or the celestial bodies returning to their orbits.

questions.text: Allah swears by the 'earth which cracks open'. What does this refer to?
questions.answer: The earth splitting to allow plants to grow.

questions.text: How is the Quran described in this Surah?
questions.answer: As a 'decisive word' (Qawlun Fasl).

questions.text: Is the Quran 'amusement' or 'frivolous'?
questions.answer: No, it is not amusement.

questions.text: What are the disbelievers doing regarding the truth?
questions.answer: They are 'plotting a plot' (Yakiduna Kayda).

questions.text: What is Allah's response to the plots of the disbelievers?
questions.answer: Allah is also 'planning a plan' (Akidu Kayda).

questions.text: What instruction is given to the Prophet (SAW) regarding the disbelievers?
questions.answer: Give them respite and leave them for a little while.

questions.text: What is the relationship between the creation of man and his resurrection in this Surah?
questions.answer: Allah uses the miracle of man's first creation from a fluid to prove that reviving him is easy for Him.

questions.text: What is 'Sada'?
questions.answer: The cracking or splitting of the earth for vegetation.

questions.text: Why are 'secrets' emphasized in this Surah?
questions.answer: To show that the judgment is not just on outward actions but on the intentions of the heart.

questions.text: Does this Surah provide comfort to the Prophet?
questions.answer: Yes, by reminding him that Allah's plan is superior to the enemies' plots.
